_comment: Machine-readable registry of every data source and redistributable dataset Golavo carries, plans, or has rejected. It is the single source of truth for attribution, license classification, and CI provenance gates. scripts/validate_sources.py cross-checks it against packs/snapshots.json and every pack manifest; scripts/gen_third_party_notices.py renders THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md from it. Contributor names are derived ONLY from an upstream LICENSE/CITATION/repository owner or a cited paper's author list, never invented. Verified dates record a live primary-source check; recheck_by marks blocked/rejected entries to revisit.

license_classes.core: CC0-1.0 / public-domain data folded into the bundled index; joins freely within the class.
license_classes.enrichment: CC-BY-4.0 factual enrichment (coords, timezones); may join core read-only for display, attribution enforced.
license_classes.odbl-pack: ODbL-1.0 share-alike data; physically isolated user-data pack, never joined to core or another class.
license_classes.by-sa-pack: CC-BY-SA-4.0 share-alike data; physically isolated pack, never joined to core, ODbL, or another class.
license_classes.research-pack: CC-BY-4.0 (or MIT) historical event/tracking data; per-match artifacts only, raw tables never join the index.
license_classes.dev-only: OSI-licensed software used only in dev/CI; never shipped in a runtime dependency set.
license_classes.rejected: Terms verified incompatible with free/open redistribution; recorded so CI can forbid its use.
license_classes.blocked: Would be usable but a live blocker (license contradiction, gated host, missing LICENSE) prevents adoption; recheck_by set.

sources.name: ClubElo
sources.fields: club Elo ratings API
sources.notes: REJECTED: publishes no license or terms of any kind. Free access is not a redistribution grant.

sources.name: Fjelstul World Cup Database
sources.fields: men's (1930-2022) + women's (1991-2019) World Cup history: matches, players, managers, referees, stadiums, events, awards
sources.attribution: Fjelstul World Cup Database v1.2.0 © 2023 Joshua C. Fjelstul, Ph.D., CC BY-SA 4.0; modifications noted.
sources.notes: Vendored as an isolated facts-only CC-BY-SA pack. Grant lives only in README+DESCRIPTION (no LICENSE file), no v1.2.0 tag — pinned at commit f942c6b with README+DESCRIPTION retained as license evidence. NEVER substitute WorldCups.ai downloads (CC BY-NC-SA). Golavo uses only the men's 1930–2022 history.

sources.name: football-data.org
sources.fields: fixtures/results API (free tier)
sources.notes: REJECTED: proprietary API service with a post-cancellation data restriction. Free access is not openly licensed redistributable data.

sources.name: footballcsv/deutschland
sources.fields: Historical completed results for the Bundesliga (1963-64 to 2009-10), a CC0 1:1 mirror of the openfootball datasets in flat CSV. Rows carry date, teams, and full-time score; no half-time score, no kickoff time (day precision).
sources.attribution: Historical the Bundesliga results from footballcsv/deutschland (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled as deep pre-2010 history, trimmed to end the season before openfootball's earliest bundled season (2010-11) so it never overlaps or duplicates the football.json rows. Its own source_id keeps it out of any forecast's training set (history and search only, seal-ineligible). Upstream is dormant; no runtime refresh adapter is wired, so the bytes move only when the pack is rebuilt.

sources.name: footballcsv/england
sources.fields: Historical completed results for the English Premier League (1992-93 to 2009-10), a CC0 1:1 mirror of the openfootball datasets in flat CSV. Rows carry date, teams, and full-time score; no half-time score, no kickoff time (day precision).
sources.attribution: Historical the English Premier League results from footballcsv/england (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled as deep pre-2010 history, trimmed to end the season before openfootball's earliest bundled season (2010-11) so it never overlaps or duplicates the football.json rows. Its own source_id keeps it out of any forecast's training set (history and search only, seal-ineligible). Upstream is dormant; no runtime refresh adapter is wired, so the bytes move only when the pack is rebuilt.

sources.name: GeoNames
sources.fields: city coordinates, country, timezone, altitude — bulk dumps (cities15000, countryInfo, timeZones)
sources.attribution: Data from GeoNames (geonames.org), CC BY 4.0.
sources.notes: Bundled as a separately registered enrichment pack pinned to the 2026-07-15 dump and exact file hashes. The compact table resolves 1,571 of 2,215 indexed city-country pairs through unique exact GeoNames primary-name matching. All 82 ambiguous pairs remain excluded; 175 alias candidates remain pending, and no manual city resolutions ship. Bulk files only, with no runtime web-service dependency.

sources.name: IDSSE — integrated spatiotemporal + event data
sources.fields: 7 elite German matches (~2.63 GB): synchronized TRACAB tracking + DFL event data (XML)
sources.attribution: IDSSE dataset, provided with authorization of the Deutsche Fußball Liga (DFL); Bassek et al., Sci Data 12:195 (2025), CC BY 4.0.
sources.notes: Research-only (pipeline validation). 2.63 GB for 7 matches — not a user-facing pack. Attribution must name DFL and cite the paper.

sources.name: martj42/international_results
sources.fields: men's senior international results 1872-present, date-only (00:00 UTC kickoff proxy), goalscorers, shootouts, former-name aliases
sources.attribution: International football results from martj42/international_results (Mart Jürisoo), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled and seal-eligible. Sole forward-seal training source. Date-only; exact kickoff requires the worldcup-json overlay.

sources.name: martj42/womens-international-results
sources.fields: women's international results (unlicensed)
sources.notes: BLOCKED: no LICENSE file as of the verified date (default all-rights-reserved). Do not use or redistribute. Recheck for an added open license.

sources.name: Meteostat
sources.fields: historical observed weather (temperature, precipitation, wind); bulk annual Parquet
sources.notes: BLOCKED: /license states CC BY 4.0 but the live /faq states CC BY-NC 4.0 — a contradiction that must be resolved in writing before adoption. Also no historical-FORECAST archive exists, so weather can only ever be context, never a backtested pre-match feature.

sources.name: Natural Earth
sources.fields: simplified public-domain vector coastlines and country boundaries for offline travel maps
sources.attribution: Made with Natural Earth.
sources.notes: Bundled as a separately registered public-domain enrichment pack pinned to Admin-0 Countries v5.1.1 at commit 9380cca and exact file hashes. The compact 1:110m GeoJSON basemap is offline; no tile service is required.

sources.name: Open-Meteo (hosted free API)
sources.attribution: Weather data by Open-Meteo.com
sources.fields: pre-kickoff weather forecast (temperature, precipitation, wind, weather code)
sources.notes: Per-user, consent-gated, keyless fetch from the user's own machine; display-only context (model_input:false), never bundled, exported, or fed to a model. The free API tier is non-commercial and its output is CC-BY 4.0; a visible 'Weather data by Open-Meteo.com' link is shown wherever a reading appears. Shown only when captured before kickoff.

sources.name: openfootball/champions-league
sources.fields: UEFA Champions League, Europa League, and Conference League main-competition results in Football.TXT format; qualifiers excluded from the bundled packs
sources.attribution: European club competition data from openfootball/champions-league (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled as three competition-isolated historical packs at commit abfaedd: Champions League 2020/21-2025/26, Europa League 2020/21-2024/25, Conference League 2021/22-2024/25. Every file passed exact declared-row, result-completeness, and format-era checks. Two cancelled Europa ties are retained in source bytes but excluded from the match index. Qualifiers are not bundled; kickoff precision is day-only because upstream local clocks have no timezone.

sources.name: openfootball/deutschland
sources.fields: Football.TXT season schedule for the Bundesliga: matchday, date, venue-local kickoff clock (no timezone token upstream), and the two sides; scores appear once played
sources.attribution: The bundesliga fixtures from openfootball/deutschland (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled and runtime-refreshable as a co-source of openfootball-football-json. Supplies fixture identity, result once published, and the local kickoff clock; unplayed rows are never training-eligible, and the clock has no timezone so kickoff_precision stays 'day'. Each runtime update is commit-pinned, hash-receipted, schedule-certified, diff-checked, and activated atomically with last-known-good fallback.

sources.name: openfootball/england
sources.fields: Football.TXT season schedule for the English Premier League: matchday, date, venue-local kickoff clock (no timezone token upstream), and the two sides; scores appear once played
sources.attribution: The english premier league fixtures from openfootball/england (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled and runtime-refreshable as a co-source of openfootball-football-json. Supplies fixture identity, result once published, and the local kickoff clock; unplayed rows are never training-eligible, and the clock has no timezone so kickoff_precision stays 'day'. Each runtime update is commit-pinned, hash-receipted, schedule-certified, diff-checked, and activated atomically with last-known-good fallback.

sources.name: openfootball/espana
sources.fields: Football.TXT season schedule for La Liga: matchday, date, venue-local kickoff clock (no timezone token upstream), and the two sides; scores appear once played
sources.attribution: La liga fixtures from openfootball/espana (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled and runtime-refreshable as a co-source of openfootball-football-json. Supplies fixture identity, result once published, and the local kickoff clock; unplayed rows are never training-eligible, and the clock has no timezone so kickoff_precision stays 'day'. Each runtime update is commit-pinned, hash-receipted, schedule-certified, diff-checked, and activated atomically with last-known-good fallback.

sources.name: openfootball/euro
sources.fields: UEFA European Championship fixtures/results in Football.TXT format
sources.attribution: European Championship data from openfootball/euro (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Registered but not bundled: the available finals editions duplicate UEFA Euro results already carried by martj42. Golavo avoids adding duplicate match rows; martj42 remains the historical international results source.

sources.name: openfootball/europe
sources.fields: Football.TXT season schedule for Ligue 1: matchday, date, venue-local kickoff clock (no timezone token upstream), and the two sides; scores appear once played
sources.attribution: Ligue 1 fixtures from openfootball/europe (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled and runtime-refreshable as a co-source of openfootball-football-json. Supplies fixture identity, result once published, and the local kickoff clock; unplayed rows are never training-eligible, and the clock has no timezone so kickoff_precision stays 'day'. Each runtime update is commit-pinned, hash-receipted, schedule-certified, diff-checked, and activated atomically with last-known-good fallback.

sources.name: openfootball/football.json
sources.fields: top-five European league files published through 2025-26; season completeness is certified per league and 2025-26 is incomplete in the audited snapshot (no 2026-27 files published as of verified date)
sources.attribution: Club football results from openfootball/football.json (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled and refreshable as the verified historical base for five competition-specific league packs. Certified current schedules can be sealed; completed club settlement still requires an independent result source. Country-repository rows carry day-precision kickoff and exact field-level co-source provenance.

sources.name: openfootball/italy
sources.fields: Football.TXT season schedule for Serie A: matchday, date, venue-local kickoff clock (no timezone token upstream), and the two sides; scores appear once played
sources.attribution: Serie a fixtures from openfootball/italy (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled and runtime-refreshable as a co-source of openfootball-football-json. Supplies fixture identity, result once published, and the local kickoff clock; unplayed rows are never training-eligible, and the clock has no timezone so kickoff_precision stays 'day'. Each runtime update is commit-pinned, hash-receipted, schedule-certified, diff-checked, and activated atomically with last-known-good fallback.

sources.name: openfootball/worldcup.json
sources.fields: World Cup fixtures/results with exact kickoff time + UTC offset, round, venue city, stadium timezone/coords (separate stadiums file); knockout placeholders W###/L### before teams resolve
sources.attribution: World Cup fixtures and kickoff times from openfootball/worldcup.json (Gerald Bauer and contributors), CC0-1.0.
sources.notes: Bundled as a fixture/kickoff co-source inside the refreshed internationals pack: supplies the World Cup fixtures martj42 has not published yet plus exact kickoff times. Excluded from model training (martj42 remains the sole training source). A user-authorized post-match check may also use its pinned, hashed final score to settle a World Cup seal when martj42 still reports NA/NA; if both sources publish and disagree, settlement fails closed. W###/L### placeholder fixtures are rejected until the bracket resolves.

sources.name: OpenLigaDB
sources.fields: Current-season Bundesliga 1/2/3 and DFB-Pokal fixture/result context with matchDateTimeUTC; community-entered
sources.attribution: Datenquelle: OpenLigaDB (www.openligadb.de) — Open Database License (ODbL) v1.0.
sources.notes: Optional isolated, per-user ODbL overlay. No response bytes ship with Golavo. The v1 adapter is display-only and allowlists the current-season bl1, bl2, bl3 and dfb identities; it cannot train models, seal or settle forecasts, enter exports, or join the CC0/CC-BY core. A redistributed adapted database must itself honor ODbL and carry attribution/license information. Single-operator hobby service — feature must tolerate its permanent absence.

sources.name: Pappalardo/Wyscout public soccer event dataset
sources.fields: 1,941 historical matches, 3,251,294 events, and 4,299 players (2017/18 big-five leagues + WC2018 + Euro2016): events, matches, players, teams, referees
sources.attribution: Event data: Pappalardo et al., Sci Data 6:236 (2019), CC BY 4.0 (collected by Wyscout).
sources.notes: Vendored isolated derived pack: seven competition-and-era team summaries covering 1,941 matches and 3,251,294 events. Never blended into live models or simulations and never labeled 'observed xG'. Raw events and player identities are not redistributed; attribution and modification notices ship inside the pack.

sources.name: SkillCorner Open Data
sources.fields: 10 A-League 2024/25 matches: broadcast tracking (10fps, extrapolated), dynamic events, phases of play, season aggregates
sources.attribution: Tracking data courtesy of SkillCorner and PySport (SkillCorner Open Data, MIT).
sources.notes: Optional research pack. Preserve documented limits (~97% ID accuracy; extrapolated frames flagged).

sources.name: StatsBomb Open Data
sources.fields: event data (restricted)
sources.notes: REJECTED: user agreement forbids redistribution and commercial use, is revocable, and mandates logo attribution. Not an open-data license.

sources.name: Wikidata
sources.fields: structured data only — stable entity ids, multilingual aliases, country/confederation, venue metadata
sources.attribution: Structured data from Wikidata (CC0); we credit it voluntarily as 'Data from Wikidata'.
sources.notes: The bundled Phase 4 context remains limited to a maintainer-reviewed QID allowlist with no runtime network. Phase 7 separately permits explicit, consent-gated runtime research through the declared API paths; those candidates remain untrusted and never trigger automatic identity merges. Structured claims are CC0; prose, sitelink text and Commons media are excluded.

sources.name: English Wikipedia
sources.fields: user-selected article text for local research evidence; no images or media
sources.attribution: Text from Wikipedia contributors, CC BY-SA 4.0; page URL and revision retained.
sources.notes: Runtime research only, off by default and user-selected. No Wikipedia bytes ship. Captures and local correction candidates remain in a physically isolated, non-exportable BY-SA namespace; media is forbidden.
